fields.description:
## Description
Foundation seam for the code_health lens: a language-agnostic analyzer protocol + a per-language *selection* helper the metric readers call instead of scanning hardcoded paths. No tool shell-outs yet — ship the protocol, the selector, and a null/default analyzer so the contract is testable in isolation (additive; changes no existing behavior).

## Review-driven corrections
- REUSE the existing `rebar.metrics.registry.Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)` sentinel (the SAME shape the CLI renders as `{reason, accruing_since}`) — do NOT invent a single-arg `Unavailable("...")`; the fallback helper returns `Unavailable(reason="no analyzer configured for <lang>", accruing_since=<ts>)`.
- Do NOT name the new concept "registry"/"REGISTRY" (collides with `metrics/registry.py` + module-level `REGISTRY`). Use e.g. `ANALYZERS` map + `resolve_analyzer(lang)`.
- New adapters (later tickets) live in a NEW `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/` package, NOT `adapters/` (which is quarantined: imported only by harvest/backfill scripts, never by core/registry).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `Analyzer` protocol + `AnalyzerResult` (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported).
- [ ] `resolve_analyzer(lang)` returns None when none configured; fallback helper returns the reused `Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)` — NEVER scans a path, fabricates zeros, or leaks an exception string.
- [ ] Pure stdlib; import registers nothing into `REGISTRY` as a side effect (opt-in wiring lands in 0734).

## Verification
- [ ] Unit: miss -> Unavailable(reason, accruing_since); support -> AnalyzerResult passthrough. `make lint` + `pytest tests/unit/metrics`.

## Dependencies: None (keystone; blocks 9597, e7a0, 18f9, 21fb, 3ba0, 0734)
## Files likely touched: src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py (new), tests/unit/metrics/test_analyzer.py (new)
## Estimated scope: M
